raw_consumer_statement:
In XX/XX/year>, I placed a {$650.00} order with XXXX XXXX XXXX. The merchant claimed to have shipped my order and provided XXXX tracking number XXXX. However, the package was never tendered to XXXX. The tracking page only ever showed Label Created/ In XXXX to XXXX with no origin scan, no acceptance scan, and no physical receipt of the package by XXXX. XXXX eventually removed the tracking because the parcel was never received. 

Despite multiple attempts to resolve with the merchant, they continued to blame XXXX, even though XXXX never took possession. The merchant never shipped the product. 

I filed a dispute with Citi, but Citi denied it, apparently accepting the merchants false claim that the package was shipped. Citi did not address the key fact : a XXXX label without an acceptance scan is not proof of shipment, and no goods were ever delivered or tendered to the XXXX. 

The merchant still shows the order as in transit on their website, confirming the item was never shipped. 

I am requesting the CFPBs assistance because Citi failed to properly investigate the dispute under the Fair Credit Billing Act. I paid for merchandise that I never received, and the merchant provided no evidence whatsoever that the item was shipped or delivered. 

I am requesting that Citi re-open and correct the dispute and issue a refund for the undelivered {$700.00} order.
